speaker: COUNTESS
text: Porter, remember what I gave in charge,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: And when you have done so, bring the keys to me.

speaker: PORTER
text: Madam, I will.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: The plot is laid. If all things fall out right,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: I shall as famous be by this exploit

speaker: COUNTESS
text: As Scythian Tamyris by Cyrus’ death.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Great is the rumor of this dreadful knight,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: And his achievements of no less account.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Fain would mine eyes be witness with mine ears

speaker: COUNTESS
text: To give their censure of these rare reports.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Madam, according as your Ladyship desired,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: By message craved, so is Lord Talbot come.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: And he is welcome. What, is this the man?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Madam, it is.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Is this the scourge of France?

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Is this the Talbot, so much feared abroad

speaker: COUNTESS
text: That with his name the mothers still their babes?

speaker: COUNTESS
text: I see report is fabulous and false.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: I thought I should have seen some Hercules,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: A second Hector, for his grim aspect

speaker: COUNTESS
text: And large proportion of his strong-knit limbs.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Alas, this is a child, a silly dwarf!

speaker: COUNTESS
text: It cannot be this weak and writhled shrimp

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Should strike such terror to his enemies.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Madam, I have been bold to trouble you.

speaker: TALBOT
text: But since your Ladyship is not at leisure,

speaker: TALBOT
text: I’ll sort some other time to visit you.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: What means he now? Go ask him whither he goes.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Stay, my Lord Talbot, for my lady craves

speaker: MESSENGER
text: To know the cause of your abrupt departure.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Marry, for that she’s in a wrong belief,

speaker: TALBOT
text: I go to certify her Talbot’s here.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: If thou be he, then art thou prisoner.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Prisoner? To whom?

speaker: COUNTESS
text: To me, bloodthirsty lord.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: And for that cause I trained thee to my house.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Long time thy shadow hath been thrall to me,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: For in my gallery thy picture hangs.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: But now the substance shall endure the like,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: And I will chain these legs and arms of thine,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: That hast by tyranny these many years

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Wasted our country, slain our citizens,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: And sent our sons and husbands captivate.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Ha, ha, ha!

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Laughest thou, wretch? Thy mirth shall turn to moan.

speaker: TALBOT
text: I laugh to see your Ladyship so fond

speaker: TALBOT
text: To think that you have aught but Talbot’s shadow

speaker: TALBOT
text: Whereon to practice your severity.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Why, art not thou the man?

speaker: TALBOT
text: I am, indeed.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Then have I substance too.

speaker: TALBOT
text: No, no, I am but shadow of myself.

speaker: TALBOT
text: You are deceived; my substance is not here,

speaker: TALBOT
text: For what you see is but the smallest part

speaker: TALBOT
text: And least proportion of humanity.

speaker: TALBOT
text: I tell you, madam, were the whole frame here,

speaker: TALBOT
text: It is of such a spacious lofty pitch

speaker: TALBOT
text: Your roof were not sufficient to contain ’t.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: This is a riddling merchant for the nonce:

speaker: COUNTESS
text: He will be here and yet he is not here.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: How can these contrarieties agree?

speaker: TALBOT
text: That will I show you presently.

speaker: TALBOT
text: How say you, madam? Are you now persuaded

speaker: TALBOT
text: That Talbot is but shadow of himself?

speaker: TALBOT
text: These are his substance, sinews, arms, and strength,

speaker: TALBOT
text: With which he yoketh your rebellious necks,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Razeth your cities, and subverts your towns,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And in a moment makes them desolate.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Victorious Talbot, pardon my abuse.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: I find thou art no less than fame hath bruited,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: And more than may be gathered by thy shape.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Let my presumption not provoke thy wrath,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: For I am sorry that with reverence

speaker: COUNTESS
text: I did not entertain thee as thou art.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Be not dismayed, fair lady, nor misconster

speaker: TALBOT
text: The mind of Talbot as you did mistake

speaker: TALBOT
text: The outward composition of his body.

speaker: TALBOT
text: What you have done hath not offended me,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Nor other satisfaction do I crave

speaker: TALBOT
text: But only, with your patience, that we may

speaker: TALBOT
text: Taste of your wine and see what cates you have,

speaker: TALBOT
text: For soldiers’ stomachs always serve them well.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: With all my heart, and think me honorèd

speaker: COUNTESS
text: To feast so great a warrior in my house.
